kept.text:
Quality starter going for 6 innings 📊

MLB POTD #2 | (5/4) ⚾️

Edward Cabrera "O" 15.5 PO (-110) | @DraftKings  
#Cubs

Backing Cabrera to go deep again here. 5 of 6 starts clearing 15.5 outs this season, 3 straight covers, and 3 of 4 at home over the line. 

Cabrera so far this season⬇️

• 3.06 ERA, 1.19 WHIP, 4.04 xFIP, 38.3% HardHit

• 86th percentile Chase%, 72nd percentile 
Fastball Velo, 63rd percentile Whiff%

• Home: 3.38 ERA, 1.21 WHIP, 3.92 xFIP

• Last 4 home starts: 6, 6, 7, 5.2 IP

The matchup tilts heavily in his favor because of how this Reds lineup is built. Cabrera’s only real weakness is vs LHB (.312 wOBA, .687 OPS, 5.58 xFIP), and Cincy is rolling out 4 potential lefties at most with Friedl, Lowe, and Elly is a switch hitter and possibly bleday added. Six righties otherwise, and Cabrera owns RHB to the tune of a .267 wOBA, .593 OPS, 2.37 xFIP.

Wind is blowing out 15 mph, which usually scares people off backing a starter, but Cabrera’s profile actually neutralizes it. He generates grounders at a steady clip, gets chases at an 86th percentile rate, and pounds the zone with a 95.8 mph heater. Most of the contact he gives up stays on the ground, which keeps the wind from being a factor and keeps his pitch count manageable enough to push into the 6th and 7th. 16.5 is barely 5.2 IP. He’s gone at least 5.2 in 5 of his last 6.

Hit rate over the last 3:

• 4/28 vs SD: 5.2 IP
• 4/23 vs PHI: 7 IP
• 4/17 vs NYM: 6 IP

Workhorse arm, righty heavy lineup, and a profile built to keep balls on the ground regardless of conditions. Everything lines up for another deep outing.

❤️ If you tailed ‼️
